title: TIN - Target Instruction Notation
description: A JSON format for specifying what an AI model should and should not do for a given role or task, including referenced materials, expected output, and metadata.

tin.description: Version of the TIN format this document conforms to.

sections.description: Ordered list of instruction sections. The heart of the TIN.

files.description: Referenced materials (local files or external URLs) with guidance on how to treat them.

related.description: Identifiers of other TINs that relate to this one.

extensions.description: Free-form object for domain-specific extensions. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields here.

id.description: Stable identifier for this TIN. Reverse-DNS style or UUID recommended.

description.description: One-line summary of what this TIN is.

purpose.description: Longer explanation of why this TIN exists and when to use it.

type.description: Kind of TIN. Common values: role, task, guideline, workflow, evaluation. Custom values allowed.

lang.description: Language of the TIN content (ISO 639-1 code, optionally with region).

version.description: Semantic version of this TIN's content.

created.description: Creation date in ISO 8601 (date or date-time).

modified.description: Last modification date in ISO 8601 (date or date-time).

tags.description: Free-form labels for categorization and search.

Context.description: Optional overall context in which this TIN applies.

assumptions.description: What is assumed about the user, environment, or inputs.

out_of_scope.description: What is explicitly NOT covered by this TIN.

id.description: Identifier of the section, unique within the TIN.

instructions.description: Individual instructions belonging to this section.

areas.description: Nested sub-sections. Same shape as Section (recursive).

type.description: Kind of instruction. 'do' = must do, 'dont' = must not do, 'note' = context/remark.

text.description: The instruction text. Markdown is allowed and will be auto-detected.

id.description: Identifier of the file, unique within the TIN.

uri.description: Location of the file. Either a relative local path or an absolute URL.

understand.description: How to understand this file - what it is and its authority.

use.description: What to take from this file and how to use it.

ignore.description: What to ignore or skip in this file.

scope.description: Free-text description of which part of the file is relevant (e.g. 'Chapter 3', 'Lines 40-120').

description.description: Free-text description of what the output should look like.

language.description: MIME-like language specifier for the output (e.g. 'text/czech', 'code/javascript', 'application/json').
